source_or_gap: The book discussed KV allocation, warm-prefix controls, page cache, and semantic descendants but did not coherently distinguish exact prefix reuse, persistent KV reuse, output memoization, semantic response caching, or honest cache-hit pricing.
chapter_action_policy: Deepen three existing owners: Fast Generation owns inference-reuse mechanisms and receipts; Resource Economics owns break-even and pricing; Context Transactions owns response validity and invalidation. Do not create a standalone cache chapter.
merge_or_split_policy: Keep numerical reuse, economics, and durable validity in their existing owners and connect them with one CacheReuseReceipt. A standalone chapter would duplicate serving, resource, and transaction boundaries.
boundary_decision_rationale: A cache hit is not one mechanism: exact KV reuse changes prefill, output memoization changes whether a model runs, semantic reuse changes decision semantics, and persistence changes state lifecycle. The existing owners already govern those consequences.
external_literature_need: periodic refresh of current provider cache contracts and prices
independent benchmark of persistent and disaggregated KV-cache implementations
semantic-cache false-positive and stale-answer benchmarks with public workloads
cache privacy, timing, poisoning, and deletion studies
proof_or_test_backlog: implement a versioned CacheReuseReceipt schema with valid, partial-hit, stale, cross-tenant, and deleted-entry fixtures
execute a natural exact-prefix workload with multiple prefix lengths and reuse counts under cold, warm, burst, and failover conditions
compare cache-aware and fairness-aware scheduling with complete tail and starvation accounting
evaluate semantic output reuse separately on deliberately confusable, dynamic, personalized, and poisoned prompts
evidence_transition_precondition: exact model, tokenizer, runtime, cache identity, capacity, lifetime, tenant, policy, and source epochs frozen prospectively
matched cache-disabled and full-prefill baselines plus source-recommended tuning
quality and exact-output checks, method-specific rescue, and independent evaluator
complete write, read, storage, transfer, lookup, eviction, miss, output, verification, and governance cost
required_before_drafting: completed: primary source inventory and notes
completed: source-scoped claims and current-price volatility boundary
promotion_blockers: no local cross-request cache deployment or billed hit
no independent reproduction of external latency or throughput results
no complete invalidation, deletion, privacy, or transfer result
provider behavior and price terms are time-sensitive
residuals: CacheReuseReceipt schema and fixtures remain unimplemented
next_action: Retain the integrated reader material and implement the exact-prefix receipt and natural workload before any cost, speed, or safety promotion.
non_claims: does not create a new chapter
does not prove a cache implementation faster, cheaper, private, or correct
does not reproduce provider pricing, bills, or service behavior
does not promote a chapter-core claim
does not authorize deployment, cross-tenant sharing, or semantic answer reuse
